Jas.2.5: Hath not God chosen the poor of this world rich in faith, and heirs of the kingdom? Divine election runs against worldly criteria. Faith-wealth eclipses material wealth.
Prov.16.9: A man's heart deviseth his way, but the LORD directeth his steps. Human planning, divine determination. The verse balances human agency with sovereign providence.
John.1.18: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, He hath declared (exēgēsato — exegeted) Him. Christ is the divine exegesis. He has explained the unseen God by being Him.
Col.1.16: By Him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible. All things were created by Him, and for Him. Christ as Creator and goal of creation.
Isa.65.24: Before they call, I will answer; and while they are yet speaking, I will hear. Eschatological intimacy. God's responsiveness anticipates prayer.
1John.3.2: When He shall appear, we shall be like Him, for we shall see Him as He is. Glorification follows beatific vision. The vision transforms.
Eph.5.2: Walk in love, as Christ also hath loved us, and hath given Himself for us, an offering and a sacrifice to God for a sweetsmelling savour. The cross as the sustained pattern of Christian love.
Isa.6.9: Hear ye indeed, but understand not — judicial hardening. Cited by Jesus (Matt 13:14) when explaining the parables. Divine judgment includes giving people what they want.
Rev.21.8: The fearful, and unbelieving, and the abominable... shall have their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death. Eschatological exclusion from the new creation. Eight categories named.
Ps.34.18: The LORD is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart, and saveth such as be of a contrite spirit. God's proximity is to the crushed, not the proud. Brokenness is the venue of salvation.
2Pet.3.9: The Lord is not slack concerning His promise — divine timing is not delay. Longsuffering, not willing that any should perish, but that all should come to repentance. The patience is purposeful.
Jer.17.10: I the LORD search the heart, I try the reins, even to give every man according to his ways. Divine omniscience for moral assessment. Judgment proceeds from comprehensive knowledge.
Isa.66.2: To this man will I look, even to him that is poor and of a contrite spirit, and trembleth at my word. Three traits attract divine attention: poverty of spirit, contrition, reverence for Scripture.
2Cor.5.10: We must all appear before the judgment seat (bēma) of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body. Believers' deeds judged for reward, not condemnation (Rom 8:1).
Luke.10.27: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy strength, and with all thy mind; and thy neighbour as thyself. The lawyer's correct summary. Both tables of the Decalogue compressed.
1John.1.9: If we confess (homologōmen — agree with God about) our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness. Confession is pre-condition; forgiveness is justly grounded in the cross.
1Pet.1.6: Wherein ye greatly rejoice, though now for a season ye are in heaviness through manifold temptations. Joy and grief co-exist for the Christian; trials are brief and purposeful.
Matt.12.34: Out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh. The mouth is heart-revelation. Speech is symptomatic, not causative.
Col.1.27: Christ in you, the hope of glory. The greatest mystery: the indwelling Christ as the down-payment of consummated glory. Personal indwelling, present tense, eschatological orientation.
Rom.10.9: If thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised Him from the dead, thou shalt be saved. Confession (objective fact: Jesus is Lord) + faith (subjective trust in resurrection).
Matt.25.34: Come, ye blessed of my Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world. Election language at the eschatological enthronement. The kingdom is pre-prepared.
Rom.8.11: If the Spirit of Him that raised up Jesus from the dead dwell in you, He shall also quicken your mortal bodies by His Spirit. Resurrection power is the Spirit's promise to indwelt believers.
Exod.15.26: I am the LORD that healeth thee. Yahweh-Rapha — one of the covenant names. Conditional on obedience; the principle of moral health, applied also to physical.
Gal.5.16: Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh. Spirit-walking is the antidote to flesh-fulfillment. The two are mutually exclusive in any given moment.
Jer.32.27: Behold, I am the LORD, the God of all flesh: is there anything too hard (yipale') for me? Rhetorical assertion of omnipotence. Echoes Gen 18:14.
Mal.3.10: Bring ye all the tithes into the storehouse... prove me now herewith, saith the LORD of hosts, if I will not open you the windows of heaven. Tithing test. The verse is OT covenantal; the principle of generous giving with God's overflowing supply remains.
2Cor.3.17: Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty. Spirit-presence produces freedom — from law's condemnation, from self, from fear.
1Pet.2.25: Ye were as sheep going astray; but are now returned unto the Shepherd and Bishop (episkopon — overseer) of your souls. Christ as both pastoral guide and oversight authority.
Gen.28.15: I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest. The covenant promise to Jacob at Bethel. Comprehensive divine companionship.
Prov.3.9: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase. Material giving as covenantal honor. The firstfruits principle.
Isa.49.23: They shall not be ashamed that wait for me. Eschatological vindication of God's people. The waiting is rewarded with public honor.
Jer.10.14: Every man is brutish (ba'ar — stupid) in his knowledge: every founder is confounded by the graven image; for his molten image is falsehood. Idolatry is the bankruptcy of human wisdom.
Col.4.6: Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man. Christian speech: gracious in tone, preserving in effect, contextually wise.
John.6.40: This is the will of Him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son and believeth on Him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day. The Father's will, the Son's promise, eschatological resurrection.
Ps.110.1: The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool. The most-quoted OT verse in the NT. Christ cites it (Matt 22:44) to confirm His own deity.
Exod.19.5: If ye will obey my voice indeed, and keep my covenant, then ye shall be a peculiar treasure (segullah) unto me above all people. Conditional covenant identity. Cited 1 Pet 2:9 of the church.
Jas.5.7: Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth. The farmer's patience as Christian model.
Isa.55.1: Ho, every one that thirsteth, come ye to the waters... come, buy and eat; yea, come, buy wine and milk without money and without price. Free salvation invitation. The auctioneer's call to the destitute.
Luke.24.44: All things must be fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me. Christ's threefold canonical claim — He is the subject of the OT.
Col.3.5: Mortify (nekrōsate — kill, put to death) therefore your members which are upon the earth. Five vices listed. Sanctification involves decisive killing of sin, not negotiation.
Zeph.3.17: The LORD thy God in the midst of thee is mighty; He will save, He will rejoice over thee with joy; He will rest in His love, He will joy over thee with singing. The astonishing portrait of God singing over His people.
Matt.6.24: No man can serve two masters... Ye cannot serve God and mammon (mamōn — material wealth). Categorical exclusivity. Money as a rival deity.
Ps.27.4: One thing have I desired of the LORD, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life. The simplified prayer of focused devotion.
Acts.20.28: Take heed unto yourselves, and to all the flock... feed the church of God, which He hath purchased with His own blood. Pastoral charge to the Ephesian elders. Particular redemption stated explicitly.
John.1.4: In Him was life; and the life was the light of men. The Word as life-source and light-source for humanity. Verse 5: the light shines in darkness, undefeated.
2Pet.1.17: He received from God the Father honour and glory, when there came such a voice... This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Peter's eyewitness of the Transfiguration.
Jer.31.31: Behold, the days come, saith the LORD, that I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel. Cited Heb 8:8–12. The new covenant inaugurated at the Last Supper (Luke 22:20).
Rev.21.4: God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain. The new creation's negative inventory — what is removed.
Hab.2.3: For the vision is yet for an appointed time... though it tarry, wait for it; because it will surely come. Eschatological waiting. Cited Heb 10:37 of Christ's coming.
Rom.8.15: Ye have not received the spirit of bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of adoption (huiothesias), whereby we cry, Abba, Father. Adoption produces filial intimacy with God.
Ps.139.23: Search me, O God, and know my heart: try me, and know my thoughts. David's invitation to divine inspection. The prayer of the believer with nothing to hide.
Heb.10.36: Ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise. Endurance bridges obedience and reward.
Rev.7.14: These are they which came out of great tribulation, and have washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb. Tribulation saints, cleansed by atoning blood. The paradox: blood whitens.
Acts.10.38: God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good. Trinitarian Christology in Peter's sermon. The Spirit-anointed mission of Jesus.
Isa.40.3: The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the LORD. Cited in all four Gospels of John the Baptist. Messianic forerunner prophecy.
Matt.10.22: Ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake; but he that endureth to the end shall be saved. Persecution prophesied; perseverance is the marker of genuine faith.
Deut.28.1: It shall come to pass, if thou shalt hearken diligently unto the voice of the LORD thy God... the LORD thy God will set thee on high above all nations. The Mosaic blessings conditional on obedience.
John.3.5: Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God. Water = spiritual cleansing (Ezek 36:25); Spirit = regeneration. Both are facets of one new birth.
1John.5.11: This is the record, that God hath given to us eternal life, and this life is in His Son. Eternal life is located in Christ; have the Son, have the life.
Rev.12.11: They overcame him by the blood of the Lamb, and by the word of their testimony; and they loved not their lives unto the death. Three weapons against the accuser: cross, confession, courage.
John.3.15: Whosoever believeth in Him should not perish, but have eternal life. Cited Numbers 21 (the brazen serpent) as Christ's typological self-reference (v. 14). Faith looks; faith lives.
Rev.2.7: To him that overcometh will I give to eat of the tree of life. Eden restored as eschatological reward to perseverers. The seven letters' promises to overcomers.
Prov.4.18: The path of the just is as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day. Progressive sanctification pictured. The Christian life is sunrise — increasing brightness.
Matt.22.37: Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind. The first and great commandment. Total-faculty love.
1John.3.22: Whatsoever we ask, we receive of Him, because we keep His commandments, and do those things that are pleasing in His sight. Effective prayer flows from obedient lives.
Jas.3.13: Who is a wise man and endued with knowledge among you? Let him shew out of a good conversation his works with meekness of wisdom. Wisdom is demonstrated, not asserted.
Ps.119.105: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path. Scripture as immediate (lamp) and longer-range (light) guidance.
Ps.34.8: O taste and see that the LORD is good. Experiential invitation. Theology requires personal sampling, not just propositional assent.
2Tim.2.15: Study (spoudason — be diligent) to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing (orthotomounta — cutting straight) the word of truth. Pastoral hermeneutics.
Ps.40.5: Many, O LORD my God, are thy wonderful works which thou hast done. Innumerable divine acts of providence. They cannot be reckoned up in order — beyond cataloguing.
Ps.100.3: Know ye that the LORD He is God: it is He that hath made us, and not we ourselves; we are His people, and the sheep of His pasture. Creation, possession, pastoral care — three theological pillars.
Titus.1.2: In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie (apseudēs), promised before the world began. Pre-temporal divine promise to the elect, guaranteed by God's nature.
Rom.13.14: Put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof. Christ as garment; flesh-provisioning is incompatible. Augustine's conversion verse.
John.7.6: My time is not yet come; but your time is alway ready. Christ's life followed a divine schedule; ours follows our own. The verse implies messianic timing.
Heb.4.13: All things are naked and opened unto the eyes of Him with whom we have to do. Divine omniscience makes accountability inevitable. Nothing is hidden.
Rev.4.11: Thou art worthy, O Lord, to receive glory and honour and power: for thou hast created all things. Creation grounds worthiness of worship. The 24 elders' creed.
Eph.2.2: Wherein in time past ye walked according to the course of this world, according to the prince of the power of the air. Pre-conversion: world-conformed and demon-influenced. The unbeliever's threefold bondage (vv. 1–3): world, flesh, devil.
Matt.20.28: The Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give His life a ransom (lytron) for many. Christ's mission summarized. Substitutionary atonement plainly stated.
Dan.7.13: One like the Son of man came with the clouds of heaven... and there was given Him dominion, and glory, and a kingdom. Jesus' favorite self-designation drawn from this verse. Coronation vision.
2Cor.13.5: Examine yourselves, whether ye be in the faith; prove your own selves. Self-examination commanded. Subjective assurance is aided by objective marks.
Matt.19.29: Every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren... for my name's sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit everlasting life. Discipleship's cost-and-reward economy.
Heb.13.21: Make you perfect (katartisai — equip) in every good work to do His will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in His sight. God both equips and operates within the believer.
Phil.2.3: Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves. The mind of Christ (v. 5) anchors humility-grounded ethics.
1Tim.4.12: Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity. Six categories of exemplary Christian conduct.
1Pet.1.14: As obedient children, not fashioning yourselves according to the former lusts in your ignorance. Conversion replaces conformity to ignorance with conformity to the Father.
2Pet.1.3: His divine power hath given unto us all things that pertain unto life and godliness, through the knowledge of Him. Sufficiency of God's resources for sanctified living.
Jas.4.4: Know ye not that the friendship of the world is enmity with God? Friendship-with-the-world is theological hostility. James's rebuke is sharp.
Mark.7.21: Out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications... Thirteen evils named. The heart, not the environment, is the source of moral pollution.
1Chr.29.11: Thine, O LORD, is the greatness, and the power, and the glory, and the victory, and the majesty: for all that is in the heaven and in the earth is thine. David's doxology. Likely the source for the kingdom-power-glory clause in the Lord's Prayer.
Isa.42.1: Behold my servant, whom I uphold; mine elect, in whom my soul delighteth. The first Servant Song. Cited Matt 12:18 of Christ.
Rev.1.8: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the ending, saith the Lord, which is, and which was, and which is to come, the Almighty. Christ's deity asserted by the Father's titles.
Deut.10.12: What doth the LORD thy God require of thee, but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him? Five-fold covenant requirement compressed.
Acts.3.19: Repent ye therefore, and be converted (epistrepsate — turn around), that your sins may be blotted out. Peter's Pentecost-aftermath sermon. Repentance + conversion + forgiveness.
Jas.4.7: Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Submission to God enables successful resistance.
Ps.147.3: He healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds. Divine pastoral care. The verse anticipates Christ's healing ministry.
1Thess.5.18: In every thing give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus concerning you. Comprehensive thanksgiving as God's revealed will.
Heb.13.15: By Him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually... the fruit of our lips giving thanks to His name. NT priesthood: spoken praise as continual offering.
Col.3.2: Set your affection (phroneite — direct your mind) on things above, not on things on the earth. Mental orientation as the wellspring of behavior.
Jer.9.23: Let not the wise man glory in his wisdom, neither let the mighty man glory in his might. The objects of human boasting are forbidden grounds. Verse 24: only glory in knowing God.
Matt.10.28: Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather fear Him which is able to destroy both soul and body in hell. Fear-prioritization. The fear of God displaces the fear of man.
Isa.35.5: The eyes of the blind shall be opened, and the ears of the deaf shall be unstopped. Messianic-age miracles. Cited by Jesus (Matt 11:5) as His own works.
2Tim.4.18: The Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto His heavenly kingdom. Paul's doxology in his last letter. Confidence in eternal preservation despite imminent execution.
Josh.24.15: Choose you this day whom ye will serve... but as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD. Joshua's covenant ultimatum. The household head's spiritual leadership.
Eccl.12.13: Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep His commandments: for this is the whole duty of man. The Preacher's verdict. Reverent obedience is the human telos.
2Thess.3.3: The Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil. Divine faithfulness as the ground of preservation.
Matt.16.27: The Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father with His angels; and then He shall reward every man according to his works. Eschatological judgment with works-based reward (for believers; Rev 20 for unbelievers).
1John.5.4: Whatsoever is born of God overcometh the world: this is the victory that overcometh the world, even our faith. Faith is the world-overcoming weapon, and regeneration produces it.
Ps.138.8: The LORD will perfect that which concerneth me. Divine completion of personal sanctification. Echoes Phil 1:6.
1Pet.1.7: That the trial (dokimion — testing) of your faith, being much more precious than of gold... might be found unto praise and honour and glory at the appearing of Jesus Christ. Tested faith's eschatological dividend.
Rom.8.14: As many as are led by the Spirit of God, they are the sons of God. Spirit-leadership is the indicator of sonship. Test of life.
2Cor.5.15: He died for all, that they which live should not henceforth live unto themselves, but unto Him which died for them and rose again. Christ's death calls for self-displacing life.
Rom.8.26: The Spirit also helpeth (synantilambanetai — joins in to help) our infirmities... the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered. Spirit-prayer beneath our prayers.
1Pet.4.7: The end of all things is at hand: be ye therefore sober, and watch unto prayer. Eschatological proximity calls for prayerful sobriety.
Isa.30.18: The LORD will wait, that He may be gracious unto you... blessed are all they that wait for Him. Even God waits — to gather the qualified recipients of His grace.
Matt.5.44: Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you. Four imperatives toward enemies. The radical Christian ethic.
2Cor.9.6: He which soweth sparingly shall reap also sparingly; and he which soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully. Generous giving's harvest principle.
Col.4.2: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving. Three present imperatives — perseverance, vigilance, gratitude — interwoven.
Ps.34.15: The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, and His ears are open unto their cry. Divine attention focused on the righteous. Cited 1 Pet 3:12.
1John.1.8: If we say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us. Self-perception of sinlessness is self-deception. Confessional Christianity vs. perfectionist illusion.
Matt.6.9: Our Father which art in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. The Lord's Prayer opens with relationship and reverence. Father (immanence) and heaven (transcendence) balanced.
Rom.8.35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress... Seven catastrophic candidates listed. Verse 37: in all these things we are more than conquerors.
Ps.29.11: The LORD will give strength unto His people; the LORD will bless His people with peace. Divine bestowal of strength and peace as covenantal benediction.
Titus.3.3: We ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived... Pre-conversion catalog. The 'we' includes the apostle. Salvation's ground (v. 4–5) is mercy, not merit.
Prov.28.26: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered. Self-trust is foolishness. Cited inferentially throughout the Reformed tradition's rejection of carnal reason.
1Cor.1.9: God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the fellowship (koinōnia) of His Son Jesus Christ. Calling is into relationship. Divine faithfulness undergirds calling's permanence.
Eph.4.1: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation (klēsis) wherewith ye are called. Calling generates corresponding ethics.
Ps.30.5: Weeping may endure for a night, but joy cometh in the morning. Sorrow's brevity vs. joy's morning arrival. The pattern of divine consolation.
1Chr.4.10: Jabez called on the God of Israel, saying, Oh that thou wouldest bless me indeed, and enlarge my coast. The unexpected prayer in the genealogy. God granted his request.
Acts.20.24: None of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry. Paul's vocational priorities. Mission outranks self-preservation.
Prov.10.22: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and He addeth no sorrow with it. Divine prosperity comes without divine grief. Riches by other means do not.
1Tim.1.15: This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief. Paul's gospel summary with self-identification as chief sinner.
Luke.21.36: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things... and to stand before the Son of man. Eschatological vigilance.
2Cor.5.7: We walk by faith, not by sight. Faith vs. sight as competing modes of orientation. Christian living suspends physical evaluation for spiritual reality.
Eph.1.18: The eyes of your understanding being enlightened; that ye may know what is the hope of His calling, and what the riches of the glory of His inheritance. Spiritual perception as the result of divine illumination.
Rev.11.15: The kingdoms of this world are become the kingdoms of our Lord, and of His Christ; and He shall reign for ever and ever. Eschatological transfer. The seventh trumpet announcement.
John.6.45: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me. Divine pedagogy precedes coming to Christ.
Ps.119.1: Blessed are the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the LORD. The longest psalm opens with a beatitude on Torah-walking. The whole psalm celebrates the Word.
Zech.12.10: They shall look upon me whom they have pierced, and they shall mourn for Him. Future Israel's recognition of the crucified Messiah. Cited John 19:37 of the cross; ultimate fulfillment at Christ's return.
Gal.5.19: The works of the flesh are manifest, which are these: adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness... Fifteen vices listed (vv. 19–21). Compare with the fruit of the Spirit (v. 22).
Gal.5.24: They that are Christ's have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts. Past-tense decisive crucifixion of flesh as the believer's positional reality.
Ps.16.11: In thy presence is fulness of joy; at thy right hand there are pleasures for evermore. Divine presence is the supreme good. Cited Acts 2:28 as fulfilled in Christ's resurrection.
Gen.1.26: Let us make man in our image, after our likeness — divine plurality (Trinitarian foreshadowing). Image-bearing grounds human dignity and dominion. The 'us' problematizes simple unitarianism.
Rev.22.17: The Spirit and the bride say, Come... whosoever will, let him take the water of life freely. The Bible closes with a free invitation. Spirit, church, and hearers join the call.
1Cor.2.14: The natural man (psychikos) receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God; for they are foolishness unto him. Spiritual things require spiritual perception. Apologetics has its limit at regeneration.
Jer.23.5: I will raise unto David a righteous Branch (tsemach), and a King shall reign and prosper. Messianic prophecy. The Branch motif (Isa 11:1, Zech 3:8) coalesces in Christ.
John.15.2: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit He taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, He purgeth (kathairei — prunes) it. Divine pruning of fruitful branches. Discipline serves productivity.
1Pet.2.12: Having your conversation honest among the Gentiles: that... by your good works, which they shall behold, glorify God. Visible Christian conduct as evangelistic apologetic.
Phil.4.4: Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice. Doubled imperative for emphasis. Joy is commanded — and located in the Lord, not in circumstance.
Rom.8.9: If any man have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of His. Possession of the Spirit is the test of Christ-belonging. No Spirit, no salvation.
Heb.6.18: By two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation. The two: God's promise and His oath (Gen 22:16–17). Double divine bond.
John.16.13: He, the Spirit of truth, is come, He will guide you into all truth: for He shall not speak of Himself. The Spirit's apostolic guidance, undergirding the New Testament's inspiration.
Matt.16.18: Upon this rock I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it. The rock is Peter's confession (v. 16) — Christ Himself. The church's invincibility guaranteed.
1Pet.1.2: Elect according to the foreknowledge (prognōsin) of God the Father, through sanctification of the Spirit, unto obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ. Trinitarian salvation. Election precedes sanctification precedes obedience.
1Pet.4.8: Above all things have fervent charity (ektenē — stretched-out love) among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins. Love's covering function — overlooking offenses.
Eccl.9.10: Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might; for there is no work, nor device... in the grave. Wholehearted labor in the brief window of life.
Matt.5.11: Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake. The eighth beatitude. Persecution-for-righteousness is blessed.
